reviews.review_text:
[h1]A few thoughts on Marvel’s Spider-Man Remastered. [/h1]

My 4-year-old son is a huge Spider-Man fan, and since the game was basically dirt cheap during a Steam sale, I picked it up thinking we’d just mess around with it a little on weekends.

We started casually: TV, Xbox controller, full chill mode. He would move from mission to mission, swing around the city and explore, while I handled most of the combat. It quickly turned into a surprisingly fun father-son gaming experience.

Then at some point I realized... I was getting seriously hooked myself and started playing even when he wasn’t around, because the game is genuinely insanely fun.

Long story short: between playing together and me continuing solo, we almost 100%-ed the game and all the DLCs (we’re only missing 3 suits), and somehow ended up spending around 50 hours in it.

And yes, “we’ll just try it for a bit” remains the biggest lie in gaming right after “just one more quest.”

I had heard the Spider-Man games were great, but honestly, I didn’t expect them to be [b]THIS [/b] good. Especially considering the original game came out in 2018. From the movement and combat to the animations and the overall feeling of New York City, everything is crafted with an absurd amount of care and attention to detail.

Huge respect to Insomniac Games. They absolutely nailed it.

If you enjoy action-adventure games and somehow still haven’t played this gem, I highly recommend it. The game is absolutely worth your time.

Also, we’re already about 2.5 hours into Marvel's Spider-Man: Miles Morales and that game already feels like a proper next step instead of just a reskin of the first one. Which honestly makes me even more excited to eventually get to Marvel’s Spider-Man 2.

But one thing at a time. Right now, me and my son are busy trying to beat Miles Morales challenges. :)

winging around New York alone makes this game worth playing.
The combat feels smooth, the story hits harder than expected, and visually it still looks amazing. Some side activities get repetitive, but honestly, being Spider-Man never stops being fun.

One of the best superhero games ever made. 🎮🕷️

Marvel's Spider-Man Remastered was an amazing experience from start to finish. The web-swinging around New York City never got old, the combat felt smooth and satisfying, and the story was genuinely engaging with some memorable emotional moments. The graphics still look fantastic, and the PC version runs very well overall.

I also went for the 100% achievement completion, which gave me even more reasons to explore everything the game has to offer. While I enjoyed completing all the content, some of the achievements felt a little repetitive since they required doing similar activities multiple times. It wasn't difficult, just a bit grindy toward the end.

The game isn't completely perfect either. I encountered a few bugs during my playthrough, such as occasional visual glitches and minor issues with NPCs, but nothing serious enough to ruin the experience.

Overall, Marvel's Spider-Man Remastered is one of the best superhero games I've played. Despite a few bugs and some repetitive achievement requirements, the fantastic gameplay, great story, and incredible traversal make it an easy recommendation.

Good to see that Mj sucks in the game, comics and the movies. No gwen stacy is a dissapointment imo

Great game otherwise.

It's still not the _best_ superhero game I've played, but it is the best traversal in any game I've played.  The writing is clever, the setting is pretty, the art is gorgeous, and the voice actors gave it their all.

Would heartily recommend.

Fun game at first, but quickly becomes tedious and repetitive. You spend most of the game beating up on the same faceless thugs over and over again. Only a few unique fights with bosses but they are few are far between with the bulk of them coming near the end of the game. The webslinging feels great definitely the highlight of the game. The city feels big but after a few hours it feels hollow. The whole thing feels like a waste of potential, they could have had more fun encounters mixed up the enemies with some minor villans or encounters that involved something other than dropping in and beating up 5 thugs. 
Over all its worth playing through the story once, I don't think i would bother trying to 100% it. I am hopeful for the rest of the series though.

the combat is great and i dig using all of the gadgets. swinging around nyc is fun. definitely recommend

Marvel’s Spider-Man Remastered was an absolute blast to play. The gameplay is incredibly fun, especially the action sequences and web-swinging stunts , they never get boring and keep you fully engaged throughout the game. Combat feels smooth, fast, and satisfying with plenty of stylish moves.

The story is also pretty decent and emotional at times, making the overall experience even better. I enjoyed it so much that I completed both the main story and all the DLCs. Definitely worth playing if you enjoy action-packed superhero games with great movement mechanics.

This is a really good game, I would 100% recommend it to any superhero fans! And for any Spidey fans, let's just say, With Great Story, Gameplay, Animation, and Epic Costumes, Comes Spider-Man Remastered!

Заранее хочется сказать, что купил ее за 15$, не по фулл прайсу. В целом, своих 15 зеленых американских она стоит, но не более, имхо.

На чем играл: геймпад. Вибрация на взаимодействие имеется.

Начнем с того, что изначально я все же задушнился прохождением после первых миссий, а потом пошел тратить время, летая над прекрасным Нью-Йорком. Город действительно красив, особенно при закате. Почему задушнился? Долго тянуть не буду и скажу: до середины сюжета вы будете, по сути, делать одно и то же. Пойди туда, узнай инфу и разберись с парой десятков врагов. Все. Сюжет раскрывается постепенно — это плюс, но все довольно скучно и однотипно (хотя для тех, кто не шарит за лор паучка, ближе к середине/концу игры многое будет непонятно). Были миссии, где решал стелс, и это были одни из лучших миссий в игре.

Боевка: первые несколько часов действительно выглядит интересно и разнообразно, но этому приходит конец, когда замечаешь однотипные анимации, даже если прокачивал свои навыки. На финальном «боссе» (без спойлеров) я просто летал и отправлял ему обратно то, чем он бросался. Базы врагов несколько раз осилил, но задушниться там быстрее всего, ибо врагов много, а жетоны для улучшения костюма нужны. Итог: легко, анскильно, казуально. Все.

Боссы: особой сложности не представляют, потому что геймплей достаточно казуальный. Вам восстановят хп, а при смерти на некоторых боссах есть сейв на середине их хп (примерно, ибо лайфбара нет). Сами боссы однотипные, все, что меняется — диалоги и сам персонаж.

«Открытый» мир. Типичный момент Ubisoft: он есть и одновременно его нет. Однотипные локации: магазины, вышки, повторяющиеся дома. Сказать нечего, просто ЮБИСОФТ.

Полет на паутине: ВОТ РЕАЛЬНО ЕБ#ЙШАЯ ЧАСТЬ. Я кайфовал от того, как вайбово можно провести время, просто летая по городу и ощущая себя свободным Человеком-пауком. Играл я на геймпаде, что придавало больше интереса к взаимодействию во время полета. Ради полета можно провести в игре дополнительные 3–4 часа.

Награды: я собрал все рюкзачки по городу, открыл все вышки и нашел убежище Черной кошки. Знаете что? Я получил пару ачивок и один вид костюма, который был даже без модификатора. Награды недостойные.

Баги: это раздражало больше всего. Невозможно было нажать кнопку (key prompt) — подсказка банально не появлялась на экране, пока ты не перезапустишь игру к черту. Баги по врагам также имеются, например, у солдатов Сейбл. Они застревали в стенах, толпились на одном месте, когда должны были проходить мимо, и много чего другого. К сожалению, рестарт станет вашим вторым другом.

Общая оценка: 7.5/10. Для фанатов будет более увлекательно, думаю, но если вы равнодушны к этой вселенной — игра на один раз, не более.

Worth playing? absolutely

worth 100%-ing?... not if you value your sanity

DLC? dealers choice: more game, but less polish & more frustration...
The story is amazing the gameplay is spectacular Its superior to any other SpiderMan game and truly feels like the ultimate SpiderMan experience
I’d rate this game around an 8/10. The story is engaging, the gameplay is solid, and the combat doesn’t feel too repetitive.

This was an AMAZING game! The story is fast paced, meaningful and has great graphics. One of the most fun things to do in this game is Web swinging any and everywhere. Its smooth and easy to use which makes it more fun to just swing around rather than use the fast travel in the game.

Game play: 8/10
Graphics: 9/10
Story: 8/10
Combat: 8/10
Bugs: 8/10

There are a few issues with the game however, in the sense that the main story was definitely quite short and I didn't really notice it until when I did New Game+ for the achivement and the entire main story only took me 5 hrs to complete. Granted I knew what to expect but for the first walkthrough going at a normal pace, the main story is maybe around 9-10 hrs. That being said, this is not a bad thing. The side missions and DLC's are quite fun and interesting in its own way.
The combat was quick simple and easy however there are certain times just as boss fights which are a bit strange/clunky. They were super easy even on the hardest difficulty and offered no challenge.

Dispite these things, its a very fun time and I would recommend to check out the game!

A near perfect example of a superhero game.  It's so great from top to bottom.  Except for the missions where you don't play as spiderman... in a spiderman game... odd choice.  Still well worth the money.
Game looks absolutely amazing. Going for 100% completion right now, and nothing has yet been tedious what so ever. Just an amazing game all around
this game has a very good story and very good graphics and the plot is perfect enough, but i dont wanna spoil anything so have fun
Great mechanics, the swinging and other web related traversal moves are smooth and it's fun to just swing around New York.

What an absolutely fantastic game!
No agenda-pushing (woke) at all, amazing gameplay, awesome mechanics (the traversal swing system alone is worth mentioning).
And as for the usual "clearing outposts" formula? Almost every outpost feels like something new: a new mission, a new conversation, or even a new boss (!!!).
This is one of those games that's absolutely worth completing 100%.
P.S. I only regret not playing it sooner.
Truly amazing game. And even though you have some similar missions throughout the game, they still seem to remain interesting and change
Great game, only disliked the silberlining dlc enemies they were annoying AF. Amazing movement and webslinging, combat very fluid and great overall feel of the game.
Great game, one day I'll eventually reach completion. Feels like being Spider-Man! Love it, 10/10, works well with Crossover 26 for Mac players just don't do a long doge (it will freeze the game)

Fun, Alot of content.
Still worth it buy it on sale.
Fun game. Very rarely ever used the fast travel function and I only used it 5 times to get the achievement. The DLC's are also fun and the story overall is easy to get pulled into and invested.
great game except for SCREWBALL CHALLENGES. WHO MADE THAT A THING. THEY ARE SO AGGRAVATING. IVE NEVER BEEN SO MAD DOING OBJECTIVES IN A GAME.

I think Norman Osborn was creating hate spiders coz at some point in the game, MJ has a genetically modified spider on her person for like a few hours and it didn't bite her but as soon as that same spider crosses paths with Miles Morales, he gets bit. kinda weird Norman. pretty fun game.
I was able to play this flawlessly on my Steam Deck. Amazing story and spectacular gameplay!

Love, love, LOVE this game. Rented it from Redbox for the PS4 years ago, and got SO OBSESSED with the game that I cried when I had to return it. After that, buying the whole game was a no-brainer. I probably have at least 1,500+ hours on this game across Steam, PS4, and PS5. 
Multiple playthroughs, multiple 100% save files. This game redefined Spider-Man entirely for me and gave me a new perspective on superheroes. This game made me the Spider-Man fanatic I am today, and Insomniac Spider-Man is *THE* Spider-Man in my eyes.
As I upgrade my computer, I'm gonna be striving to 100% this game, Miles Morales, and Spider-Man 2 again on Steam. Just because I love these games and the joy they've brought me. 
Needless to say, I absolutely recommend this game.

The scenery, the iconic Spider-Man villains and sidekicks, the animation, music, fighting gameplay… Oh man, there is so much good in this game that mesh to a blast of a game. Only some of the gap filler mission weren't that much of fun, to even the point of being buggy like opponents blocked in a wall. Also some of the crewball mission and especially the final against here were just bad game design. 
Still over all a great game.
I put this game down for over a month before coming back to it, took almost no time at all for me to pick the controls and story back up.
Best game i played back in the summer of 2023. Holds a lot of good memories and honestly this is just a game that will never leave me. When i had nothing i had this game, Truly a life changer.
Spectacular, Amazing, Sensational, there are too many ways to describe this game, it almost feels like an amazing fantasy

8/10 

Gameplay: Excellent game mechanics, great varied open world feel and well-designed level areas. Both movement/exploration and combat mechanics are intuitive and immersive. Play with a controller with good haptic feedback. Well worth it! The circuitry levels weren't to my taste but that's a minor quibble since those are tiny. 

Visuals and sensory immersion: Good quality. It's not chasing uber photorealism, but it does get pretty close especially considering that it works well on 6 year old budget specs. The haptics really help. 

Story, characters and voice acting: _chefs kiss_. The emotional stakes really, really shine here. Great characters with unique personas, facial expressions are executed well and realistically enough (in the sense that it registers as a person -- many games can get realistic features but poor jaw movements).
Best Spiderman game ever!!! Amazing storytelling and spectacular fighting mechanics!

The more you play, the more fun you'll have. The combat and webslinging around the city take a few hours to get the hang of when you're new to the game, but eventually you'll level up the skill tree and also just getter a better feel for the movement, abilities and combat timing. It's a blast by the time you reach act 2.

the controls can be infuriating at times, shooting webs when you dont intend to, webbing to a location you dont intend, often in the opposite direction you're going. it can be really f*cking annoying. but when it works its amazing.
The game is great the controls are easy to get used to and the graphics are good. What more could you ask for except part 2?
Smoothe combat, beautiful open world game with a decent story. Worth playing
Its very good game with good story mode .but sometimes enemies get glitchout and have other bugs which are not fix yet.

Genuinely one of my favorite super-hero stories, a genuinely refreshing approach to a classic hero that's genuinely exciting to replay every time. I love pointing to this game when the "web shooters vs organic webs" debate comes around - the gadgets in this game are FUN. They allow you to get creative in the way you [strike] kill [/strike] take down bad guys.

instant classic, can't believe it's technically almost 8 years old. That being said, this is an incredible PC port for a game intended for PS4/5.

One of the best purchases I've made so far on steam. I've put so many hours into this game within just a few months lol. Almost complete with the main campaign and I can't wait to try out the DLC included. I might actually do the new game plus as well lol

Combat feels great, and reminds me of the old school spider man games on the OG Xbox days. 
The Playstation port isn't horrible but it is annoying at times, usually when you're trying to executions or combos they sometimes won't work. And when you rely on it to save you or not take damage for a brief moment it can suck, causing you to die. 
Otherwise great game and good studio, will definitely buy the other 2 games. 
Overall, 8/10
Already 100% on PS, just needed to do it again on PC. STILL amazed at how well Insomniac did with this series. Absolutely LOVE this game. 9/10

An absolutely amazing game from to start to finish! A legendary opponent to the Arkham series! One of the first games to ever make me cry. Actually the reason I got a PS4 back in 2018. So bringing this to PC was a stroke of pure genius, not only because it allowed people who couldn't get it, but also the hardware advances they could do with PC. But of course it goes without saying one of its strengths is the modding talents of the community. 

I have downloaded countless versions of the same suit

The DLC is also phenomenal with side missions that hold so much narrative weight and a curvy goddess in black leather who I would die for.

It's a really fun game ngl. Completing it does feel like a chore at times since it feels a bit repetitive in terms of district activities but it's still mostly fun to do. Especially when you try to complete the objectives. The story is pretty amazing too and I loved it. DLC was nice but felt a bit lacking or short.

This game truly is wonderful and captures the essence of Spider-Man perfectly. His quips, the combat, the web swinging and everything is expertly captured and done and as a Spider-Man fan it was truly wonderful to watch and see the evolution of this superhero throughout the story that was so wonderfully crafted. Seeing the Sinister Six was also a great surprise, the boss fights were pleasant utalizing different gameplay mechanics to make each one unique to the specific villain. The narrative and dialogue choices in this game are superb. All the DLC are also amazing add-ons with perfect stories that add onto the main game. My favourite was The Heist and Silver Lining but that's because I love Black Cat and she adds a different dynamic when working with Spider-Man and I liked Silver Sable and Spiderman together alot. I also enjoyed the various activities in NYC like the: backpacks, landmarks, crimes, enemy bases it added alot of variety and made getting the trophies super enjoyable after 100% the base game, all the 'City never sleeps' DLCs I think this game is truly amazing. Defo reccomend.

Buggy, glitchy mess, full of crashes. You can't enter a building without it giving up.
Would recommend

The gameplay is great at first and feels very fluid and there are tons of options to be creative with combat. However, there is very little enemy and activity variety, which make the game feel repetitive after about 5 hours. There are new and cool gadgets to unlock but they are all pretty similar and don’t change too much. The story is also amazing, but the miles and MJ sections are a drag.
it is so much fun even when you played the first one before remastered  and you get the 3 dlc it is the best

what a great spide man game, the combat feels amazing, the web shooters are awesome, all the gadgets have different use to them. the mechanic of the game reminds me of a batman arkham knight which was absolutely fire, not to mention the breathtaking views of nyc, swinging on your web around the buildings, getting sky high is just feels so good. the story to this is also fantastic, it's like im a kid again watching a spider man movie, the characters are amazing and the villains as well. overall a must cop game in you library

I like the Arkham games better especially with the combat but this game is alright. The MJ missions were awful, why the hell would I buy a Spider-Man game to have to play as a defenseless woman that has an attitude problem and thinks her ♥♥♥♥♥♥ job as a journalist is more important than whatever her superpowered boyfriend is doing to save the city? I mean the game is called "Spider-Man" not "nagging ♥♥♥♥♥♥ journalist ginger chick" ffs.
its good the plot and everything(especially the black cat one), you need good specs, and if you're new to the game maybe go for medium dif.
one of the most engaging games ive ever played, 100%ed it twice and cant wait to open miles morales for the first time (runs great, some big lag spikes when theres lots of dead enemies tho)
this game reminded me of why I loved the og spider man games on xbox back in the day. Cant wait to play miles morales and spiderman 2
it's my favorite open world game currently and, although the web swinging could be better, it is still my favorite!

10/10 First off this game has a great story I mean it was perfect its part playing a game part watching a spider man movie!! The web swinging physics are amazing like truly I spent a LOT of time just swinging around the city when I first got the game. The boss battles are equally fun and challenging. The spider-sense feature is a great addition it makes it so its not just like any other games combat. I also wanted to point out the graphics are really good and I've haven't found any bugs. The feature to be able to unlock suits is cool too I love all the different variants. As of writing this I just finished the story and am in the process of unlocking all the suits as well as completing all the districts. Once again totally recommend 10/10.

As someone who grew up playing the Batman Arkham games, this game has been extremely great to play through. The story, the voice acting, the gameplay (oh, the gameplay), it's all fantastic. Swinging around the city, shooting your webs everywhere, is an absolute blast. Speaking of swinging and shooting webs, many random crime events happen throughout the city, ranging from muggings, car chases, shootouts, and robberies. We also can't gloss over the boss fights. While, in my opinion, pretty easy, they are very fun. I'll never forget the cinematic moment of beating Vulture to death with my bare hands. Oh, the blood, the screams, they're all coming back to me. That feeling of holding his fragile heart in my hands as I watched it pump its last ounce of blood. That feeling is indescribable. I can feel it crawling. Crawling beneath my skin like some kind of parasite. Perhaps the heart I necrotized that day was none other than my own. What is a man without a heart? A nuisance, that's what he is. A nuisance who doesn't deserve to see another light of day. You think I don't know what I've done? You don't know anything. You don't know what's out there in the swamp! Hear my name, I am the angel of death, the son of Caliban, the opera mortalis, and you will heed my warning. Anyways, the only part of the game I didn't care much for were the boring as hell MJ and Miles missions, but those were still pretty good for story development.

SPIDERMAN PS4: 
Genuinely a perfect game. Such a great story. Loved every minute of it, and I can only speak praise of this game. Hope more people would play this and give it the praise it deserves, Perfect story with great casting. Yuri Lowenthal is a great Spider-Man VA, and his voice fits the role so well. LET'S GO LESBIANS!!!! If you haven't tried this game before, you're doing yourself a disservice. It's peak.

CTNS:
I have played through this once and I don't want to touch it ever again.

Pretty good game after not playing any Spiderman game since The Amazing Spiderman back in the day. The mobility can get a little boring after awhile, so the fast travel is definitely something that I utilized. One major issue that I had was when I was pulling up to a random crime event for Sable jetpackers, at least one of the jetpackers would glitch through a building making impossible to complete the crime event. Other than that, it was a pretty good game. 7.2/10

The game feels refreshing and is quite interesting on the storyline i would definitely suggest to buy and experience atleast once also make sure the graphics is set to the ultra so that you would get the best and nicer quality to experience
Compelling game and great visuals. You definitely need a powerful graphics card to get the full benefit though. Definitely recommend!
The Best spiderman game I ever played and it's hard to admit that as an avid ♥♥♥♥ rider of the old spider man one and two games I really thought nothing would ever top those for me lol

ABSOLUTELY AMAZING!!!

Easily one of my favourite open-world game's ever. Took me 35 hrs to complete 85% of the game (full story and most side objectives and side missions). 

Likes:
- Repeatative side objectives like "clear this area of enemies" are always fun to play.
- Dialogue and character interactions feel natural in the whole game and doesn't seem forced.
- Characters have depth and don't feel empty.
- Spiderman swinging and gadget gameplay loop is cool and works way better than expected. FUN!
- Story has emotional impact and actually resonates with people.
- 

Dislikes:
- The side missions are at times just stale and boring to do.
- The last bossfight wasn't didn't feel good from gameplay perspective. It was also short compared to other bossfights.
- The animations in-game sometimes start to lag or stutter after playing for sometime, but do get resolved after pausing and resuming the game.
- Should have done more with the Sinister Six story-wise.
- VSync issues exist in the game (at least I experienced it). Enabled Vsync to get rid of screen tearing, then the game menu became quite noticibly slow.

Recommendations & Tips:
- The controls would or might feel difficult at first, but overtime you will get the hang of it. So, stick with it and don't drop the game fast.
- I don't have to say this, but this feels best with Controller obviously (I used a xbox controller).
- Don't rush the game.
- If you like spider-man, BUY THE GAME. Won't regret it.

It was very nice to play this game as the graphics, movements of the main protagonist(Spiderman) and its main story with expansion is really commendable. Surely I would like to play this again if some new stories or modes will be added further in future. It runs very smooth which makes your play superb and best. With my laptop specs, low settings with a little bit of tinkering and upscaling by FSR with a resolution of 720p made my experience go boom.

Insomniac's Marvel Spider-Man Remastered is easily one of the best superhero games ever made in recent years.

The web swinging is the heart of this game and easily the part I enjoyed the most. It was the main reason I wanted to play this game in the first place, and Insomniac absolutely delivered. Swinging through the streets of New York feels immersive, cinematic, and unbelievably addictive. It felt like I was actually Spider-Man. The movement animations are incredibly smooth, making every swing, dive, and landing feel satisfying and natural.

The combat is fluid, fast-paced, and incredibly cinematic, perfectly capturing Spider-Man’s agility and fighting style. The smooth fight animations, stylish combo mechanics, aerial combat, gadgets, perfect dodge system, and environmental interactions all blend together seamlessly to make every encounter feel dynamic and satisfying. Instead of repetitive button mashing, the combat rewards creativity, movement, timing, and improvisation, making you truly feel like an experienced Spider-Man straight out of the MCU.

The story was honestly one of the biggest surprises for me. Insomniac made a smart choice by using an experienced Peter Parker instead of another teenage origin story, which made the characters and emotional moments feel much more mature and impactful. The game also does an amazing job exploring Spider-Man’s world with multiple villains and side characters without making any of them feel wasted. From Doctor Octopus and Mister Negative to Miles, Aunt May, MJ, and Yuri, everyone feels important to the story and Peter’s journey.

One real drawback for me was that some of the side quests and activities started feeling repetitive after a while, but honestly, the main story, traversal, and combat were so enjoyable that it never became a major issue.

This isn’t just a great Spider-Man game — it’s one of the most immersive, cinematic, and enjoyable superhero experiences ever made.

9/10 — truly makes you feel like Spider-Man.

"Mom, can we get Batman?"
"We have Batman at home."
Good game, but basically just a worse version of Batman Arkham city/knight. Web swinging is cool and there's some other things to make it different enough, but it's not on the same level.

8.5/10

what is there to say about this game everything is perfect the combat and the story are peak

The good:
The story of this Spiderman game is pretty decent and swinging through manhattan is a fun time. The visuals and voice acting for the cast are all top notch.

The Bad:
MARY JANE MISSIONS ARE TERRIBLE. AS ARE MILES MORALES MISSIONS. I got this game to be spiderman, not mary jane watson, girl reporter, or Miles Morales, currently unpowered civilian.

The other bad stuff about this game is primarily that it doesn't make you FEEL like Spiderman. He rarely ever uses super strength (a guy with a pipe can completely stop his punches), you need to unlock the ability to jump more than 5 feet off the ground, He moves like a dude that knows parkour, not a man with superhuman speed and agility.

The controls for swinging are unintuitive. It is always a coin toss if you will slam against a wall or stick to it. 

I may be being a bit unfair to this game as I am comparing it to the masterpiece of the Spiderman games that came from the tobey macguire spiderman movies. The combat, webswinging, and stories for the games were all phenomenal. Who could'be thought that swinging controls could have felt natural?

Overall, I'd give it a 7.5/10. Pretty average.
So much fun. Great mechanics, I loved swinging around the city. It brought me back to my childhood watching the original Spider-Man films

Look, you don't need me to tell you this game is good, but it is. Playing as Spider-man has been fun since the PS1. This game's combat is awesome, it has a great story, and it captures the feeling of being a superhero very well in my opinion. While i did find the few (I think four) short sections where you play as other characters jarring, it was also a fun switch up in the gameplay. This is an easy recommend for anyone from me. You a gamer? play this one.

The puzzles are fun and well-designed, although they can feel a bit repetitive at times. 
The combat system is truly one of a kind, chaining combos, juggling enemies in the air, and mixing gadgets into fights feels incredibly satisfying once you get the hang of it.

I highly recommend playing on Amazing difficulty. It’s not too hard if you spend some time doing side missions to level up and upgrade your gadgets and abilities.

I barely used fast travel during my entire playthrough because swinging around NY is just too enjoyable. Shooting webs, swinging through the buildings, doing tricks on the way to missions, and gaining some extra XP makes traveling part of the fun instead of a chore.

The combat is definitely the strongest part of the game. Once you master the combat system and learn how to combine gadgets with your combos, the gameplay becomes extremely fun and fluid.

The game also has a great photo mode, with a wide variety of camera settings 
and visual effects to play around with. 

And of course, Mary Jane always manages to call right in the middle of a fight... 
Women, right?!

Great game, 5 stars.
I've heard there are bugs but I didn't experience any during my 42 hours and basic completion of all campaigns.
It ran really good, no major lag unless I was downloading/updating something.

100% Marvel's Spider-Man Remastered on 7/26/2023. Fun game, no missable achievements, requires 1 replay in NG+ on Hardest difficulty, but if you save those for last, the replay takes no time at all because you are so powerful and can skip every side mission as only the main story matters on replay.

Was a lot of fun! Very good game to just chill out. I'm not usually an AAA game kinda girl, but this one clicked, maybe because it has that spider guy.

I think the game has a weird issue of bosses being held off for a long time. Like, the act 3 twist should have come sooner in the game. But only for gameplay reasons. But there ARE bosses if you go sidequesting.

Still one of the best spiderman adaptations out there. Runs circles around the second game. 

This might be sony's best PC port. Back in the day was running it no problem at 1080p high on a 1060. Currently able to run this game at 4k Native on a PC that could barely run Spider-man 2 above 50 fps.

Also for all you freaks out there, this game has native steam input support! Which means you can bind the extra buttons on the steam conrtroller to specific game actions instead of just emulating other buttons. And has native gyro support!

Movements feel incredibly polished.

I've loved Spider-Man Remastered since the original PS4 release, and getting it on PC just made a great game even better. Whatever criticisms people throw at the sequel are valid, but the first Insomniac Spider-Man genuinely took everything that worked from previous Spider-Man games and refined it into something that finally felt right. Even small touches like the Scorpion mission with the poison gas messing up your web-swinging were a clever nod to those older Spider-Man games where traversal was limited, and stuff like that shows real love for the source material. I'll admit I still prefer the original PS4 face for Peter and the MJ and Miles stealth missions were completely unnecessary, but those are minor gripes. Swinging through New York is still one of the best traversal systems in any game, the combat flows beautifully, and the whole thing genuinely feels like being Spider-Man. A modern superhero classic.

If you play in hardest mode " spectacular" and try not to rush the game, get in the flow of spidermans movements, learn combos, spend time to understand what different metrics mean and how to upgrade, then you will have a lot of fun playing this game.

So I'm like around halfway through the game, at the part where a certain character death happens. I get softlocked. I can't move or exit this mission at all. Fine. Maybe my save file got corrupted, stuff happens. I delete my save file and start over. Play through the whole half way again, get to the same part, softlocked again. Can't do anything.
I uninstall the game, delete all my files, delete everything. Replay the mission, still softlocked.
Genuinely the most upsetting experience I've had with a game. Literally cannot progress the story at all. I've tried everything, if you know of any solutions, please let me know, but for now, I can't in good faith give this game a good review.
